stem: An engineer takes 15 measurements of fuel level using a fuel gauge. The measurements have a mean of 29.5 % and a standard deviation of 4.0 %. However, calibration tests reveal that a fuel gauge systematically underestimates the true value by between 2.4% and 8.3%. Estimate the true fuel level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 29.5 %. After correcting for the 5.4% bias: ≈ 31.2 %. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (2.4%-8.3%) and sampling variability: [28.1, 34.3]. Point estimate: 31.2 %.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
